    "BEIR",
    "NarrativeQA"
  ],

    "Llama3 8B",

}

{
  "GPT51_suggested_dataset": [

    "OpenStreetMap POI",

  ],

}

{
  "GPT51_suggested_dataset": [
    "TOFU",
    "Pile Subset",
    "WikiText103"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_model": [
    "LLaMA2-7B",
    "Mistral-7B",
    "GPT-J-6B"
  ],

    "Perplexity",
    "Exact Match",

    "Accuracy"

  "GPT51_suggested_pipeline": "- Select a base model (e.g., LLaMA2-7B) and freeze its full parameters.\n- Construct a small forget set from TOFU and a large retain set from Pile Subset and WikiText103.\n- Compute influence scores of forget examples on model outputs or loss using influence functions or gradient-based approximations.\n- Attach a parameter-efficient adapter (e.g., LoRA) and initialize reweighting parameters based on influence scores.\n- Optimize the adapter to minimize performance on the forget set while regularizing to preserve performance on the retain set.\n- Evaluate forgetting using Exact Match and ROUGE-L on forget tasks and check increased perplexity on forget samples.\n- Evaluate general capabilities using perplexity and Accuracy on standard retained benchmarks from WikiText103 and Pile Subset.\n- Compare with baseline unlearning methods (e.g., full fine-tuning, naive erasure) using the same metrics to assess stability and efficiency."
}

}

content.text:
{
  "GPT51_suggested_dataset": [
    "FB15k-237",
    "NELL-995",
    "ConceptNet"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_model": [
    "GPT-4.1",
    "Llama 3",
    "R-GCN"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_evaluation_metric": [
    "MRR",
    "Hits@10",
    "F1"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_pipeline": "- Preprocess: Clean and split each knowledge graph (e.g., FB15k-237, NELL-995) into train/valid/test triples and precompute candidate reasoning paths.\n- Path scoring: Train or fine-tune a path-scoring module (e.g., R-GCN or a small Transformer) to assign importance scores to paths connecting query entities.\n- Path selection: For each query, select top-K highest-scoring paths and convert them into textual or structured prompts.\n- LLM reasoning: Feed selected paths plus query into the LLM (GPT-4.1 or Llama 3) to predict missing entities or relations and to generate natural language rationales.\n- Baselines: Run LLM reasoning with (a) no paths, (b) all available paths, and (c) random paths for comparison.\n- Evaluation: Measure link-prediction quality with MRR and Hits@10, and measure explanation quality/consistency with F1 on rationale-related annotations or proxy tasks.\n- Analysis: Compare accuracy, interpretability (via rationale quality), and efficiency (tokens, latency) across path-selection strategies to assess benefits of prioritizing important paths."
}

content.text:
{
  "GPT51_suggested_dataset": [
    "OpenDialKG",
    "DuRecDial"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_model": [
    "GPT-4.1",
    "Llama-3",
    "T5-Large"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_evaluation_metric": [
    "BLEU",
    "ROUGE",
    "BERTScore",
    "EntityF1",
    "KnowledgeF1"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_pipeline": "- Prepare Data:
  - Select a KG-grounded dialogue dataset (e.g., OpenDialKG, DuRecDial) with its associated knowledge graph.
  - Create two versions of the data: (a) original entities, (b) anonymized entities in both dialogue history and KG (consistent ID mapping).

- Model Setup:
  - Choose baseline LLMs (e.g., GPT-4.1, Llama-3, T5-Large) and define a KG-augmented dialogue generation setup (e.g., retrieve relevant KG subgraph per turn and feed as context).

- Training / Prompting:
  - For each model, run two conditions: with-original-entities and with-anonymized-entities.
  - Either fine-tune (for open models) or use few-shot prompting (for API models) under both conditions.

- Inference:
  - Generate responses on a held-out test set for both conditions, using the same retrieval and decoding settings.

- Evaluation (Attachment to Knowledge):
  - Compute EntityF1 and KnowledgeF1 by matching mentioned entities/relations in responses against the gold KG facts used for each turn.
  - Optionally measure hallucination rate by counting entities not in the retrieved KG.

- Evaluation (Response Quality):
  - Compute BLEU, ROUGE, and BERTScore against reference responses.
  - Optionally add human ratings for coherence and naturalness on a sampled subset.

- Analysis:
  - Compare attachment-to-knowledge metrics between anonymized vs original conditions.
  - Verify that response-quality metrics do not substantially degrade under anonymization while attachment increases."
}

content.text:
{
  "GPT51_suggested_dataset": [
    "MedQA",
    "MedMCQA",
    "PubMedQA",
    "MIMIC-III"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_model": [
    "Llama 3",
    "Med-PaLM 2",
    "GPT 4"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_evaluation_metric": [
    "Accuracy",
    "Exact Match",
    "F1",
    "ROUGE-L"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_pipeline": "- Pretrain or select a strong medical base LLM (e.g., Med-PaLM 2 or Llama 3 adapted on medical text).\n- Collect and preprocess benchmark datasets (MedQA, MedMCQA, PubMedQA; optionally structure notes from MIMIC-III for context-rich tasks).\n- Implement synthetic query generation: use a powerful teacher LLM to generate diverse, clinically realistic questions and contexts from unlabeled medical corpora.\n- Apply the MuSeR framework: iteratively refine model outputs using self-critique, multi-perspective reasoning (e.g., differential diagnosis, guideline-check, safety check), and automatic error detection on synthetic and real queries.\n- Perform query-guided knowledge distillation: have the student LLM mimic the teacher’s reasoning chains and answers on synthetic queries, weighting training by query difficulty and medical relevance.\n- Fine-tune the student LLM jointly on (a) real benchmark training splits and (b) synthetic query-answer-reasoning triples under the MuSeR + distillation regime.\n- Evaluate on held-out test sets of MedQA, MedMCQA, and PubMedQA using Accuracy, Exact Match, F1, and ROUGE-L for rationales; optionally run human clinician assessment on a subset for safety/context-awareness.\n- Run ablations: (1) baseline fine-tuning only, (2) + synthetic queries, (3) + MuSeR only, (4) + query-guided distillation only, (5) full MuSeR + synthetic + distillation; compare metrics to quantify improvements in medical context-awareness and overall performance."
}

- Evaluation:
  - Compute Accuracy, F1, ROC-AUC, and MCC per trait and per dataset

  - Compare LLM zero-shot performance vs RoBERTa-large baseline and across prompting strategies"
}

{
  "GPT51_suggested_dataset": [
    "TrustHub",
    "ISCAS85",
    "ISCAS89",
    "ITC99",
    "EPFL"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_model": [
    "GCN",
    "GraphSAGE",
    "GAT",
    "PPO",
    "Llama-3"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_evaluation_metric": [
    "Evasion Rate",
    "Attack Success",
    "Area Overhead",
    "F1 Score"
  ],
  "GPT51_suggested_pipeline": "- Preprocess
  - Convert TrustHub and benchmark circuits to uniform netlist graphs with area annotations.
  - Split into train/validation/test sets with balanced Trojan and benign examples.

- Train GNN Detector
  - Train a GCN/GraphSAGE/GAT classifier on netlist graphs to detect Trojans.
  - Tune for high F1 Score on validation data.

- Define RL Environment
  - Represent state as current netlist graph plus detector logits and area stats.
  - Define actions as subnetlist rewrite operators (gate substitution, logic restructuring, re-wiring) constrained by functional equivalence.
  - Incorporate Llama-3 to propose or rank rewrite templates and transformation macros.

- RL Optimization
  - Use PPO to learn a policy that sequences rewrites to minimize detector confidence while penalizing Area Overhead.
  - Reward = weighted combination of misclassification (evasion) and negative area increase.

- Adversarial Generation
  - Run the trained PPO agent with LLM guidance on held-out test netlists.
  - Generate multiple adversarial variants per original circuit.

- Evaluation
  - Measure Evasion Rate and Attack Success of adversarial netlists against the trained GNN.
  - Compute Area Overhead relative to original netlists.
  - Report F1 Score of the detector before and after the attack on the test set."
}

}

    "ScanNet",

}
